meta_title: For Founders: analytics setup to Solve no project ideas (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome no project ideas. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Analytics Setup to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Stop guessing and start building. Learn how to leverage analytics to discover profitable SaaS ideas with this expert-led guide using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: Most founders struggle with ideation because they build in a vacuum. By instrumenting your MVP with high-fidelity analytics, you transform user behavior into a roadmap. Don't invent problems; observe the friction points in your existing prototypes. Use Antigravity and Claude Code to automate data collection and turn signals into revenue.
content.intro:
The blank screen is the graveyard of ambition. You stare at the cursor, waiting for a 'million-dollar idea' to materialize, while your competition is already shipping. The truth is, ideas don't come from divine inspiration; they are discovered in the messy, unoptimized workflows of your target users. If you don't know what to build, it's because you aren't watching where people are currently failing.

This guide shifts your focus from creative guessing to data-driven discovery. We will implement a robust analytics layer that treats every user interaction as a data point, revealing exactly where you should pivot or double down. Stop building for yourself and start building for the friction you can measure.

steps.title: Define Your North Star Metrics
steps.description: Before writing code, identify the three key events that prove user value. Focus on conversion, retention, and time-to-value.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current project structure and propose a schema for tracking 'Time-to-Value' events for a B2B SaaS dashboard. Include event names, properties, and the business logic behind each.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Create a tracking-schema.json file based on the identified North Star metrics'.

steps.title: Inject Analytics Middleware
steps.description: Wrap your core services in an analytics decorator to capture user intent without cluttering your business logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor my API service to include a centralized analytics middleware that logs user actions to an external provider without blocking the main thread.

steps.title: Automate Funnel Visualization
steps.description: Use automated scripts to generate funnel reports, highlighting where users drop off during the onboarding process.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a script that pulls data from my analytics provider and identifies the top 3 friction points in my current user onboarding flow.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Generate a funnel-analysis.md report based on the last 30 days of event logs'.

steps.title: Identify Unmet Needs via Search Queries
steps.description: Log search terms that return zero results—this is your goldmine for new features or entire new product ideas.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Implement a logging mechanism for failed search queries in my application that flags them for review in a 'Missing Features' dashboard.

steps.title: Implement Automated Feedback Loops
steps.description: Trigger an automated prompt for qualitative feedback precisely when a user hits a high-friction event identified in Step 3.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a conditional trigger that sends a personalized feedback request to users who experience a specific error state more than twice.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Deploy feedback-loop-trigger to the error-handler module'.

pro_tips: Track 'rage clicks' as a high-intent signal for features users wish existed but can't find.
Never track everything; focus on 'actionable' data that directly informs your next product iteration.
Use cohort analysis to see if your 'no idea' phase is actually a 'wrong audience' problem.
Automate your weekly analytics summary to be sent to your email; don't rely on manual dashboard checks.
Tag your events with session-context to correlate bugs with specific feature usage patterns.
content.next_steps: Once your analytics are live, wait for 100 unique user sessions. Export the 'failed search' and 'high-friction' reports. Use those specific data points as the requirements for your next micro-SaaS pivot.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical infrastructure to stop guessing and start building products that users are begging for. Don't let your code sit idle—turn your data into your next revenue stream. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
